evidence: We have been increasingly using AI tools in our operations, research and development and other areas.

AI is already changing the way we and our competitors do business by, among other things, accelerating research and development, creating efficiencies, improving supply chain, productivity and other processes
We have been increasingly using AI tools in our operations, research and development and other areas.

AI is already changing the way we and our competitors do business by...accelerating research and development, creating efficiencies, improving supply chain, productivity and other processes, customer experience, talent management and decision-making.

The use of AI by us, our employees or any of our third-party partners may create risks, such as unauthorized disclosure of personal data, privacy risks, trade secrets, or confidential information, and potential receipt or use of third-party proprietary data

AI may also weaken IP rights, where AI-generated inventions or creations may not be properly attributed to the rightful inventors, potentially resulting in additional disputes over intellectual property ownership
Any failure to capitalize on the AI benefits to the same degree or with the same speed as our competitors may put us in a disadvantageous position.

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI spend, headcount, or named vendor/infrastructure partnerships disclosed in the excerpts; only general references to increasing AI tool usage.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Named use-case areas (R&D, supply chain, talent management) are mentioned but no deployment status, metrics, or quantified outcomes are provided; language remains largely aspirational/descriptive.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present and contains detailed company-specific AI risk scenarios with mechanisms (IP loss, data privacy, competitive disadvantage, cybersecurity amplification), warranting a score of 4; no quantified risk treatment or named governance bodies found to justify a score of 5.
